text: When you write code, you will almost certainly encounter bugs. The first step in debugging is identifying where the problem occurs. Instead of guessing, use a systematic approach: start by checking the most recent changes you made. Then, use debugging tools like breakpoints, which pause your program so you can inspect its state. Another helpful strategy is logging, where you insert print statements to track variable values. By following a clear process and using the right tools, you can solve problems more quickly and avoid making the same mistakes again. Remember, patience is key.
